remember: Students will be able to recall the names and positions of elements in groups 15-18.
Students will be able to identify the electronic configuration and type of hybridization in the p-block elements.
Students will be able to list the general properties of p-block elements, including electronegativity, ionization energy, and electron affinity.
understand: Students will be able to explain the trends in the properties of p-block elements across a period and down a group.
Students will be able to describe the postulates of the periodic table and how they relate to the p-block elements.
Students will be able to understand the concept of post-transition metals, metalloids, and nonmetals in the p-block elements.
apply: Students will be able to apply knowledge of the periodic table to predict the properties of elements in groups 15-18.
Students will be able to use the periodic trends to predict the reactivity of p-block elements with other elements.
Students will be able to design experiments to test hypotheses about the properties of p-block elements.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the relationships between the electronic configuration, hybridization, and properties of p-block elements.
Students will be able to evaluate the trends in the properties of p-block elements and explain their implications.
Students will be able to analyze the role of p-block elements in the formation of molecules and their applications.

ncert_objectives: Explain the general characteristics and trends in the properties of p-block elements.
Discuss the postulates of the periodic table and its significance in the context of p-block elements.
Describe the electronic configuration and hybridization of p-block elements and their role in determining the properties.
board_exam_objectives: To identify the position of elements in groups 15-18 in the periodic table.
To explain the trends in the properties of p-block elements across a period and down a group.
To describe the characteristics and applications of post-transition metals, metalloids, and nonmetals in the p-block elements.
